{
  "extract_plan": {
    "section_map": [
      {"heading": "Abstract", "location": "Abstract"},
      {"heading": "Introduction", "location": "Introduction"},
      {"heading": "Results / MACHINE LEARNING TECHNOLOGY READINESS LEVELS (Levels 0-9)", "location": "Results"},
      {"heading": "Discussion", "location": "Discussion"},
      {"heading": "EXAMPLES: Human-machine visual inspection", "location": "Examples/Human-machine visual inspection"},
      {"heading": "EXAMPLES: Computer vision with real and synthetic data", "location": "Examples/Computer vision"},
      {"heading": "EXAMPLES: Accelerating scientific discovery with massive particle physics simulators", "location": "Examples/Particle physics"},
      {"heading": "EXAMPLES: Causal inference & ML in medicine", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
      {"heading": "EXAMPLES: AI for open-source space sciences", "location": "Examples/Space sciences"},
      {"heading": "BEYOND SOFTWARE ENGINEERING", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
      {"heading": "RELATED WORKS", "location": "Related Works"},
      {"heading": "LIMITATIONS, RESPONSIBILITIES, and ETHICS", "location": "Limitations, Responsibilities, and Ethics"},
      {"heading": "CONCLUSION", "location": "Conclusion"},
      {"heading": "Methods: Gated reviews", "location": "Methods/Gated reviews"},
      {"heading": "Methods: TRL Cards", "location": "Methods/TRL Cards"},
      {"heading": "Methods: Risk mitigation", "location": "Methods/Risk mitigation"},
      {"heading": "Methods: Non-monotonic, non-linear paths", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
      {"heading": "Methods: Evolving people, objectives, and measures", "location": "Methods/Evolving people"},
      {"heading": "Methods: Quantifiable progress", "location": "Methods/Quantifiable progress"},
      {"heading": "Methods: Communication and explanation", "location": "Methods/Communication"},
      {"heading": "Methods: Robustness via uncertainty-aware ML", "location": "Methods/Uncertainty-aware ML"},
      {"heading": "References", "location": "References"}
    ],
    "concept_inventory": [
      "MLTRL framework", "Technology Readiness Level (TRL)", "technology maturity", "gated reviews", "TRL Cards", "ethics checklist", "switchback mechanisms", "discovery switchback", "review switchback", "embedded switchback", "non-monotonic/non-linear development", "technical debt", "data readiness", "data governance", "verification and validation (V&V)", "requirements documentation", "risk quantification", "sim-to-real gap", "data-oriented architecture (DOA)", "probabilistic ML", "uncertainty estimation", "aleatoric uncertainty", "epistemic uncertainty", "metamorphic testing", "golden dataset", "shadow testing", "canary testing", "data drift", "concept drift", "hidden feedback loops", "model availability", "underspecification", "valley of death", "lingua franca", "research-caliber code", "prototype-caliber code", "product-caliber code", "confounding bias", "sensitivity analysis", "identifiability", "consistency", "causal inference", "synthetic data", "domain randomization", "active learning", "federated learning", "model cards", "training-serving skew", "threat vectors", "poisoning attack", "membership inference", "interpretability", "explainability", "OKRs and KPIs", "working group", "key decision points", "data logging", "ML Testing Rubric", "CAMS", "Unity Perception", "SHERPA simulator", "risk formula"
    ]
  },
  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c-technology-maturity", "name": "technology maturity", "aliases": ["TRL maturity", "readiness level"], "description": "The maturity of a model, algorithm, data pipeline, or software module, represented by a TRL.", "grounding_span": "A TRL represents the maturity of a model or algorithm", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-gated-reviews", "name": "gated reviews", "aliases": ["gated review"], "description": "Dedicated review periods at the end of each level that graduate or hold back a technology.", "grounding_span": "The anatomy of a level is marked by gated reviews", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-trl-cards", "name": "TRL Cards", "aliases": ["TRL Card", "report cards"], "description": "Information-dense report-card deliverables tracking a technology's maturity and implicit knowledge.", "grounding_span": "these TRL Cards are key for the progression of projects", "location": "Methods/TRL Cards"},
    {"id": "c-ethics-checklist", "name": "ethics checklist", "aliases": ["AI ethics checklist"], "description": "An organization-defined checklist run through at each review to surface ethical concerns.", "grounding_span": "each review runs through an AI ethics checklist deﬁned by the organization", "location": "Methods/Gated reviews"},
    {"id": "c-switchback", "name": "switchback mechanisms", "aliases": ["switchback", "switchbacks"], "description": "Explicit purpose-driven mechanisms that regress the maturity level of specific components.", "grounding_span": "we make them explicit with “switchback mechanisms” to regress the maturity\nof speciﬁc components in an AI system", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"id": "c-discovery-switchback", "name": "discovery switchback", "aliases": [], "description": "A switchback triggered when new technical gaps are discovered through systems integration.", "grounding_span": "Discovery switchbacks occur as a natural mechanism", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"id": "c-review-switchback", "name": "review switchback", "aliases": [], "description": "A switchback resulting from a gated review dialing components back to earlier levels.", "grounding_span": "Review switchbacks result from gated reviews", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"id": "c-embedded-switchback", "name": "embedded switchback", "aliases": [], "description": "A predefined switchback path built into the MLTRL process (e.g., 4 to 2, 9 to 4).", "grounding_span": "Embedded switchbacks are predeﬁned in the MLTRL process.", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"id": "c-non-monotonic", "name": "non-monotonic non-linear development", "aliases": ["cyclic paths"], "description": "Development that dials components back to lower maturity levels rather than progressing linearly.", "grounding_span": "We observe many projects beneﬁt from cyclic paths, dialing components of a technology back to a lower level.", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"id": "c-technical-debt", "name": "technical debt", "aliases": [], "description": "Accumulated future cost from rushed or undisciplined ML development.", "grounding_span": "The lack of diligence can\nlead to technical debt", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c-data-readiness", "name": "data readiness", "aliases": ["data-readiness"], "description": "Whether data is suitable and available for a given ML task at each stage.", "grounding_span": "considers data\nreadiness and availability", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "c-data-governance", "name": "data governance", "aliases": [], "description": "How data is obtained, managed, used, and secured by the organization.", "grounding_span": "data governance:\nhow data is obtained, managed, used, and secured by the organization", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-vnv", "name": "verification and validation", "aliases": ["V&V"], "description": "Documented verification measures and validation steps tied to requirements.", "grounding_span": "veriﬁcation and validation\n(V&V) steps", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-requirements-doc", "name": "requirements documentation", "aliases": ["requirements document"], "description": "Formal documentation of technical and product requirements with V&V steps.", "grounding_span": "the formal research requirements document", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-risk-quantification", "name": "risk quantification", "aliases": ["risk-quantification"], "description": "Diligent calculation and ranking of risks across requirements, treated as first-class.", "grounding_span": "MLTRL also speciﬁes that risk quantiﬁcation\nand testing strategies are required for sim-to-real development", "location": "Methods/Risk mitigation"},
    {"id": "c-sim-to-real", "name": "simulation-to-reality gap", "aliases": ["sim-to-real gap"], "description": "The non-trivial gap in transferring a model from a simulation testbed to the real world.", "grounding_span": "the simulation-to-reality gap", "location": "Examples/Computer vision"},
    {"id": "c-doa", "name": "data-oriented architecture", "aliases": ["DOA"], "description": "A streaming-based architecture making data flows explicit and traceable, an alternative to microservices.", "grounding_span": "data-oriented architecture (DOA)", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "c-probabilistic-ml", "name": "probabilistic ML", "aliases": ["probabilistic machine learning"], "description": "Bayesian methods that naturally represent and manipulate uncertainty about models and predictions.", "grounding_span": "methods in the class of probabilistic\nML, which naturally represent and manipulate uncertainty about models and predictions", "location": "Methods/Uncertainty-aware ML"},
    {"id": "c-uncertainty-estimation", "name": "uncertainty estimation", "aliases": ["uncertainty quantification"], "description": "Valuable but hard-to-implement measures of confidence and sensitivity in AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Uncertainty estimation is valuable in many AI scenarios, yet not straightforward to implement in practice.", "location": "Examples/Human-machine visual inspection"},
    {"id": "c-aleatoric", "name": "aleatoric uncertainty", "aliases": [], "description": "Uncertainty measuring the noise inherent in the observations.", "grounding_span": "aleatoric uncertainty, measuring the noise inherent in the observations", "location": "Methods/Uncertainty-aware ML"},
    {"id": "c-epistemic", "name": "epistemic uncertainty", "aliases": [], "description": "Uncertainty accounting for uncertainty in the model itself.", "grounding_span": "epistemic uncertainty, accounting for uncertainty in the model itself", "location": "Methods/Uncertainty-aware ML"},
    {"id": "c-metamorphic-testing", "name": "metamorphic testing", "aliases": [], "description": "A software engineering methodology testing relations between outputs of multiple inputs.", "grounding_span": "Metamorphic testing: a software engineering methodology for testing a speciﬁc set of relations between the\noutputs of multiple inputs.", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-golden-dataset", "name": "golden dataset", "aliases": [], "description": "A defined dataset baselining the performance of each model and succession of models.", "grounding_span": "A “golden dataset” should be deﬁned to baseline the performance of each model", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-shadow-testing", "name": "shadow testing", "aliases": ["shadow mode"], "description": "Running models in shadow mode to stress-test infrastructure ahead of deployment.", "grounding_span": "Running models in shadow mode for a period of time would help stress test the infrastructure", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-canary-testing", "name": "canary testing", "aliases": [], "description": "Proactive and gradual testing for changing ML methods and data.", "grounding_span": "canary testing, which enable proactive and gradual testing for changing ML\nmethods and data", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-data-drift", "name": "data drift", "aliases": [], "description": "Deviations in input data distributions requiring monitoring after deployment.", "grounding_span": "Monitoring for data\nquality, concept drift, and data drift is crucial", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-concept-drift", "name": "concept drift", "aliases": [], "description": "Deviations in the underlying concept the model predicts, requiring monitoring.", "grounding_span": "concept drift, and data drift is crucial", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-hidden-feedback-loops", "name": "hidden feedback loops", "aliases": [], "description": "Real-world systems influencing their own training data via evolving user behavior.", "grounding_span": "Hidden feedback loops can be common and problematic in real-world systems inﬂuencing their own training\ndata", "location": "Examples/Human-machine visual inspection"},
    {"id": "c-model-availability", "name": "model availability", "aliases": [], "description": "Limits on deployed model access in on-premises, edge, or scale-constrained settings.", "grounding_span": "Model availability can be limited in many deployment settings", "location": "Examples/Human-machine visual inspection"},
    {"id": "c-underspecification", "name": "underspecification", "aliases": [], "description": "A key obstacle to reliably training models that behave as expected in deployment.", "grounding_span": "help mitigate underspeciﬁcation in ML pipelines, a key obstacle to reliably training models that\nbehave as expected in deployment", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-valley-of-death", "name": "valley of death", "aliases": [], "description": "The deep-tech transition where decision-makers struggle to allocate resources to productize.", "grounding_span": "sometimes referred to as “the valley of death” because project managers and decision-makers struggle", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-lingua-franca", "name": "lingua franca", "aliases": ["common nomenclature"], "description": "A shared nomenclature for AI maturity enabling cross-team and cross-org communication.", "grounding_span": "establishes a much-needed lingua franca for the AI ecosystem", "location": "Conclusion"},
    {"id": "c-research-caliber", "name": "research-caliber code", "aliases": [], "description": "Quick-and-dirty research code where full test coverage is discouraged.", "grounding_span": "Code is research-caliber", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-prototype-caliber", "name": "prototype-caliber code", "aliases": [], "description": "Robust, well-architected code covered by unit and integration tests, still pre-productization.", "grounding_span": "Code becomesprototype-caliber", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-product-caliber", "name": "product-caliber code", "aliases": [], "description": "Deployment-grade code with precise specifications, comprehensive tests, and well-defined APIs.", "grounding_span": "bring the code up to\nproduct-caliber", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-confounding-bias", "name": "confounding bias", "aliases": [], "description": "Bias in causal models addressed via propensity score matching adjustment.", "grounding_span": "confounding bias adjustment. The standard approach is to\nemploy propensity score matching to remove such bias", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
    {"id": "c-sensitivity-analysis", "name": "sensitivity analysis", "aliases": [], "description": "Analysis of how sensitive causal predictions are to violations of untestable assumptions.", "grounding_span": "Sensitivity analysis– As causal estimates are based on generally untestable assumptions", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
    {"id": "c-identifiability", "name": "identifiability", "aliases": [], "description": "Whether a causal question is identifiable from the specified causal structure plus data.", "grounding_span": "whether the causal question of interest\nis identiﬁable from the causal structure speciﬁed for the model", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
    {"id": "c-consistency", "name": "consistency", "aliases": [], "description": "Whether a learned causal estimate provably converges to the true effect at infinite sample size.", "grounding_span": "the learned causal estimate provably converges to the true causal\neffect in the limit of inﬁnite sample size", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
    {"id": "c-causal-inference", "name": "causal inference", "aliases": [], "description": "A field quantitatively making sense of cause and effect from purely observational data.", "grounding_span": "a ﬁeld known as causal inference that\ncan quantitatively make sense of cause and effect from purely observational data", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
    {"id": "c-synthetic-data", "name": "synthetic data", "aliases": ["synthetic datasets"], "description": "Generated data used to isolate features, augment scarce cases, and accelerate pipelines.", "grounding_span": "the ability to generate synthetic datasets for\nanomaly detection can accelerate the level 6-9 pipeline", "location": "Examples/Human-machine visual inspection"},
    {"id": "c-domain-randomization", "name": "domain randomization", "aliases": ["structured domain randomization"], "description": "Technique for generating diverse synthetic images to complement real data.", "grounding_span": "with structured domain randomization", "location": "Examples/Computer vision"},
    {"id": "c-active-learning", "name": "active learning", "aliases": [], "description": "Techniques leveraging unlabeled data to increase robustness of an ML subsystem.", "grounding_span": "we looked to active learning", "location": "Examples/Space sciences"},
    {"id": "c-federated-learning", "name": "federated learning", "aliases": [], "description": "Advanced privacy-oriented ML approach for limited or encrypted data access.", "grounding_span": "advanced ML\napproaches such as federated learning", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-model-cards", "name": "model cards", "aliases": [], "description": "Google-proposed cards for external user-readiness; too simplistic alone for complex systems.", "grounding_span": "model cards as demonstrated by Google for basic image classiﬁcation", "location": "Discussion"},
    {"id": "c-training-serving-skew", "name": "training-serving skew", "aliases": [], "description": "Divergence between training and serving inputs that monitoring must catch to trigger retraining.", "grounding_span": "monitoring must be enabled to catch training-serving skew", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-threat-vectors", "name": "threat vectors", "aliases": [], "description": "New ML-specific attack surfaces across the deployment workflow absent in ordinary software.", "grounding_span": "ML opens up new threat vectors across the whole deployment workﬂow", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "c-poisoning-attack", "name": "poisoning attack", "aliases": [], "description": "An attack to contaminate the training phase of ML systems.", "grounding_span": "a poisoning attack to contaminate the training phase of ML systems", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "c-membership-inference", "name": "membership inference", "aliases": [], "description": "An attack to determine whether a data record was part of the model's training.", "grounding_span": "membership inference\nto see if a given data record was part of the model’s training", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "c-interpretability", "name": "interpretability", "aliases": [], "description": "A desired but hard-to-deliver feature for domain experts to interpret predictions and models.", "grounding_span": "Interpretability is often a desired feature yet difﬁcult to deliver and validate in practice.", "location": "Examples/Particle physics"},
    {"id": "c-explainability", "name": "explainability", "aliases": [], "description": "Explaining AI at model and systems granularity, gated by MLTRL for deployment sufficiency.", "grounding_span": "MLTRL also acts as a gate for interpretability and explainability", "location": "Methods/Communication"},
    {"id": "c-okrs-kpis", "name": "OKRs and KPIs", "aliases": ["objectives and key results", "key performance indicators"], "description": "Industry progress metrics that can be defined as achieving readiness levels in a period.", "grounding_span": "industry-standard “objectives and key results” (OKRs) and “key performance\nindicators” (KPIs)", "location": "Methods/Quantifiable progress"},
    {"id": "c-working-group", "name": "working group", "aliases": ["evolving working groups"], "description": "The team owning a technology, which evolves in composition across MLTRL levels.", "grounding_span": "evolving working groups", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "c-key-decision-points", "name": "key decision points", "aliases": [], "description": "Specific points in the lifecycle where path-forward or switchback decisions are made.", "grounding_span": "calling out speciﬁckey decision points", "location": "Methods/Gated reviews"},
    {"id": "c-data-logging", "name": "data logging", "aliases": [], "description": "Logging of statistical properties of input features and model predictions unique to ML systems.", "grounding_span": "Data logging is unique in the context of ML systems", "location": "Results"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "def-requirement", "term": "requirement", "verbatim_text": "A requirement is a singular documented physical or functional need that a particular design, product, or process aims to satisfy.", "normative_status": "academic", "grounding_span": "A requirement is a singular documented physical or functional need that a particular design, product, or process aims to satisfy.", "location": "Results (footnote iv)"},
    {"id": "def-trl", "term": "Technology Readiness Level", "verbatim_text": "Technology Readiness Level (TRL)is a systems engineering\nprotocol for deep tech", "normative_status": "academic", "grounding_span": "Technology Readiness Level (TRL)is a systems engineering\nprotocol for deep tech", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "def-doa", "term": "data-oriented architecture (DOA)", "verbatim_text": "DOA aims to make the data ﬂowing between elements of business logic", "normative_status": "industry", "grounding_span": "DOA aims to make the data ﬂowing between elements of business logic", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "def-aleatoric", "term": "aleatoric uncertainty", "verbatim_text": "aleatoric uncertainty, measuring the noise inherent in the observations", "normative_status": "academic", "grounding_span": "aleatoric uncertainty, measuring the noise inherent in the observations", "location": "Methods/Uncertainty-aware ML"},
    {"id": "def-epistemic", "term": "epistemic uncertainty", "verbatim_text": "epistemic uncertainty, accounting for uncertainty in the model itself", "normative_status": "academic", "grounding_span": "epistemic uncertainty, accounting for uncertainty in the model itself", "location": "Methods/Uncertainty-aware ML"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "claim-lowest-level", "claim_text": "The TRL of a system is the lowest level of its constituent parts.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the TRL of the system is the\nlowest level of its constituent parts", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "claim-spacecraft-derived", "claim_text": "MLTRL is derived from the processes and testing standards of spacecraft development.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "MLTRL is derived from the processes and testing\nstandards of spacecraft development", "location": "Conclusion"},
    {"id": "claim-trl-nasa", "claim_text": "TRL is standard process and parlance in NASA and DARPA.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "TRL is standard process and parlance in NASA", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "claim-roberts", "claim_text": "None of the surveyed ML COVID-19 models are sufficient for clinical use due to methodological flaws and/or underlying biases.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "none of the models are sufﬁcient for clinical use due to methodological ﬂaws\nand/or underlying biases", "location": "Examples/Human-machine visual inspection"},
    {"id": "claim-netflix", "claim_text": "The Netflix $1M Prize solution was never fully deployed because of significant engineering costs in real-world scenarios.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the Netﬂix “$1M Prize” solution\nwas never fully deployed because of signiﬁcant engineering costs in real-world scenarios", "location": "Examples/Computer vision"},
    {"id": "claim-correlation", "claim_text": "Correlation is not causation, so correlation-based predictions cannot be confidently used for cause and effect.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "correlation is not causation", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
    {"id": "claim-ml-tech-debt", "claim_text": "ML systems almost necessarily increase technical debt.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "ML systems almost necessarily increase\nthe technical debt", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "claim-refactoring", "claim_text": "Package-level refactoring suffices to remove technical debt in software systems but not in ML systems.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "package-level refactoring is generally sufﬁcient for removing technical debt in software systems, but\nthis is not the case in ML systems", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"id": "claim-cams-showers", "claim_text": "The CAMS system discovered ten new meteor showers.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "discovered ten new meteor showers", "location": "Examples/Space sciences"},
    {"id": "claim-pilot-checklist", "claim_text": "MLTRL is analogous to a pilot's checklist, not autopilot.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "MLTRL is\nanalogous to a pilot’s checklist, not autopilot.", "location": "Limitations, Responsibilities, and Ethics"},
    {"id": "claim-no-skip", "claim_text": "No technology can skip levels after the MLTRL process has been initiated.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "no technology\ncan skip levels after the MLTRL process has been initiated", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"id": "claim-most-not-level0", "claim_text": "Most projects do not start at Level 0.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "most projects do not start at Level 0", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"}
  ],
  "instruments": [
    {"id": "instr-ml-testing-rubric", "name": "ML Testing Rubric", "owner": "Google", "year": 2017, "method": "scorecard for ML production readiness and technical debt", "grounding_span": "a scorecard like the ML Testing\nRubric", "location": "Results"},
    {"id": "instr-cams", "name": "CAMS (Cameras for Allsky Meteor Surveillance)", "owner": "NASA", "year": 2010, "method": "CCTV camera network with ML meteor detection", "grounding_span": "The CAMS (Cameras for Allsky Meteor Surveillance) project [51], established in 2010 by NASA", "location": "Examples/Space sciences"},
    {"id": "instr-unity-perception", "name": "Unity Perception", "owner": "Unity AI", "year": 2021, "method": "synthetic dataset generation for perception-based ML", "grounding_span": "we leveraged the Unity Perception [34] package, a toolkit for\ngenerating large-scale datasets for perception-based ML training and validation", "location": "Examples/Computer vision"},
    {"id": "instr-sherpa", "name": "SHERPA simulator", "owner": "CERN / particle physics community", "year": 2009, "method": "stochastic particle physics event generation simulator", "grounding_span": "particle physics domain\nexperts at CERN are using the SHERPA simulator[ 38], a 1 million line codebase", "location": "Examples/Particle physics"}
  ],
  "measures": [
    {"id": "measure-risk", "text": "risk = p(f ailure) × value", "response_type": "formula", "grounding_span": "risk = p(f ailure) × value", "location": "Methods/Risk mitigation"}
  ],
  "standards": [],
  "frameworks": [
    {"id": "mltrl", "name": "Machine Learning Technology Readiness Levels (MLTRL)", "owner": "Alan Turing Institute (Lavin et al.)", "year": 2021, "grounding_span": "Machine Learning Technology Readiness Levels (MLTRL)", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "trl", "name": "Technology Readiness Level (TRL)", "owner": "NASA", "year": 2003, "grounding_span": "Technology Readiness Level (TRL)is a systems engineering", "location": "Introduction"}
  ],
  "edges": [
    {"type": "builds_on", "from_id": "mltrl", "to_id": "trl", "grounding_span": "MLTRL deﬁnes technology readiness levels (TRLs) to guide and communicate AI and ML development and deployment.", "location": "Results"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "mltrl", "to_id": "c-gated-reviews", "grounding_span": "The anatomy of a level is marked by gated reviews, evolving working groups,\nrequirements documentation with risk calculations, progressive code and testing standards, and deliverables such as\nTRL Cards (Figure 3) and ethics checklists.", "location": "Results"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "mltrl", "to_id": "c-requirements-doc", "grounding_span": "The anatomy of a level is marked by gated reviews, evolving working groups,\nrequirements documentation with risk calculations", "location": "Results"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "mltrl", "to_id": "c-risk-quantification", "grounding_span": "requirements documentation with risk calculations, progressive code and testing standards", "location": "Results"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "mltrl", "to_id": "c-trl-cards", "grounding_span": "deliverables such as\nTRL Cards (Figure 3) and ethics checklists", "location": "Results"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "mltrl", "to_id": "c-ethics-checklist", "grounding_span": "deliverables such as\nTRL Cards (Figure 3) and ethics checklists", "location": "Results"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "mltrl", "to_id": "c-switchback", "grounding_span": "we make them explicit with “switchback mechanisms” to regress the maturity\nof speciﬁc components in an AI system", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c-discovery-switchback", "to_id": "c-switchback", "grounding_span": "Discovery switchbacks occur as a natural mechanism", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c-review-switchback", "to_id": "c-switchback", "grounding_span": "Review switchbacks result from gated reviews", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c-embedded-switchback", "to_id": "c-switchback", "grounding_span": "Embedded switchbacks are predeﬁned in the MLTRL process.", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c-aleatoric", "to_id": "c-uncertainty-estimation", "grounding_span": "aleatoric uncertainty, measuring the noise inherent in the observations", "location": "Methods/Uncertainty-aware ML"},

    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-spacecraft-derived", "grounding_span": "MLTRL is derived from the processes and testing\nstandards of spacecraft development", "location": "Conclusion"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-trl-nasa", "grounding_span": "TRL is standard process and parlance in NASA", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-roberts", "grounding_span": "none of the models are sufﬁcient for clinical use due to methodological ﬂaws\nand/or underlying biases", "location": "Examples/Human-machine visual inspection"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-netflix", "grounding_span": "the Netﬂix “$1M Prize” solution\nwas never fully deployed because of signiﬁcant engineering costs in real-world scenarios", "location": "Examples/Computer vision"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-correlation", "grounding_span": "correlation is not causation", "location": "Examples/Causal inference"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-ml-tech-debt", "grounding_span": "ML systems almost necessarily increase\nthe technical debt", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-refactoring", "grounding_span": "package-level refactoring is generally sufﬁcient for removing technical debt in software systems, but\nthis is not the case in ML systems", "location": "Beyond Software Engineering"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-cams-showers", "grounding_span": "discovered ten new meteor showers", "location": "Examples/Space sciences"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-pilot-checklist", "grounding_span": "MLTRL is\nanalogous to a pilot’s checklist, not autopilot.", "location": "Limitations, Responsibilities, and Ethics"},

    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "technology-readiness-levels-for-machine-learning-systems-mlt", "to_id": "claim-most-not-level0", "grounding_span": "most projects do not start at Level 0", "location": "Methods/Non-monotonic paths"},

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "MLTRL can be used in synergy with the existing, industry-standard software engineering practices such as agile [58]\nand waterfall", "suggested_edge": "complements", "from_hint": "mltrl", "to_hint": "agile and waterfall software development methodologies", "note": "MLTRL is described as complementary to / usable in synergy with existing SWE methodologies; no edge type captures 'complements/synergizes with'."},
    {"grounding_span": "switching back regresses the technology’s maturity level (e.g. from level 5 to level 3) such that it must again fulﬁll the\nlevel-by-level requirements, evaluations and reviews", "suggested_edge": "regresses_maturity_of", "from_hint": "c-switchback", "to_hint": "c-technology-maturity", "note": "A switchback regresses/lowers the maturity level of a component; no directional 'regresses' edge exists."},
    {"grounding_span": "MLTRL directly\naddresses this challenge by stepping through the technology transition or handoff explicitly.", "suggested_edge": "mitigates", "from_hint": "mltrl", "to_hint": "c-valley-of-death", "note": "MLTRL mitigates/addresses the valley-of-death challenge; no 'mitigates' relationship in the schema."},
    {"grounding_span": "with a streaming-based architecture rather than the micro-service architectures that are\nstandard in software systems", "suggested_edge": "alternative_to", "from_hint": "c-doa", "to_hint": "microservice architecture", "note": "Data-oriented architecture is positioned as an alternative to microservice architectures; no 'alternative_to' edge type."}
